# Sustainable Hypertrophy Program — Returning Lifter Edition

**Built for:** 35yo, 6'2", 186 lb, ~20% BF, detrained, joint/tendon-sensitive, home gym, busy dad (flexible 3 days/week).
**Goal:** build size while leaning to 10-15% BF, avoid injury, sustainable long-term.
**Duration:** 4-week mesocycles, cycled indefinitely with small adjustments.

## 1. The Core Philosophy

Four principles drive every decision in this program. If you understand these, you can intelligently modify anything later.

**1. Tendons adapt slower than muscles.** This is the single most important fact for you. Your muscles will "remember" how to grow within weeks of retraining — but your tendons need 8-12 weeks of consistent, progressive loading to catch up. Every time you've crashed in the past, it's because your muscles outran your tendons. The fix: moderate loads, higher reps (8-20), slow eccentrics, and capping weekly load increases at ~5-10%.

**2. Rep range doesn't determine hypertrophy — proximity to failure does.** The Schoenfeld and Lopez meta-analyses (2017-2022) confirm loads from 30%-85% of 1RM all produce equal muscle growth when sets are taken to within 0-3 reps of failure. You can build every bit as much muscle doing sets of 12-20 as sets of 5-6 — and you can do it without the joint stress that historically stops you.

**3. Volume per muscle group per week is the primary growth lever.** The target for trained individuals is 12-20 hard sets per muscle per week (Baz-Valle 2022). For you right now (detrained), we start at the low end (~10 sets) and ramp up over 4 weeks. Overshooting volume in week 1 is how returning lifters wreck themselves.

**4. Consistency > optimization.** A 70% program done 95% of the time crushes a 95% program done 60% of the time. Your A/B flex approach is smart. We're keeping it.

## 2. Program Structure

3 sessions per week, alternating Workouts A and B. Workout C is an optional swap — use it instead of a scheduled A or B when you're fried, short on time (<40 min), or joints feel off. Do not add C on top of A and B in the same week unless you're feeling great.

You're not locked to specific days. Examples: Mon/Wed/Fri fine; Tue/Thu/Sat fine; 3-day gap fine; missed a week — restart that week, don't jump forward.

Session target: 45-60 min but split however you need. Hardest compound first while fresh → smaller compounds → isolations last.

Cadence within a session: Main compound 2-3 min rest; isolation 60-90 sec rest. Each session: 1 compound push, 1 compound pull, 1 leg movement, plus targeted accessories.

## 3. The Workouts

Notation: Sets × Reps. RIR = Reps In Reserve. Target RIR: 2-3 on compounds, 1-2 on isolations. Never grind to zero on compound lifts. Tempo "3-0-1-0" = 3 sec down, no pause, 1 sec up, no pause. Slow eccentrics are a gift to your tendons.

See `mesocycle1.md` for exact prescribed weights/reps/RIR by session.

### Workout A — Horizontal Push + Quad Emphasis

| # | Exercise | Sets × Reps | Tempo | Rest | Starting Weight |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Incline DB Bench Press (elbows ~45°) | 3 × 8-12 | 3-0-1-0 | 2 min | 30-35 lb/hand |
| 2 | Chest-Supported DB Row | 3 × 8-12 | 2-1-1-0 | 2 min | 35-40 lb/hand |
| 3 | BB Back Squat (to parallel, NOT below) | 3 × 8-10 | 3-0-1-0 | 2 min | 95-105 lb |
| 4 | Lat Pulldown | 3 × 10-15 | 2-0-1-1 | 90 sec | 90-100 lb |
| 5 | DB Romanian Deadlift | 2 × 10-12 | 3-0-1-0 | 90 sec | 35-45 lb/hand |
| 6 | DB Lateral Raise | 3 × 12-20 | 2-1-1-0 | 60 sec | 10-12 lb |
| 7 | DB Curl | 2 × 10-12 | 2-0-1-0 | 90 sec | 20-25 lb/hand |
| 8 | Triceps Pushdown (rope) | 2 × 10-15 | 2-1-1-0 | 90 sec | 20-30 lb |
| 9 | Hanging or Lying Leg Raise | 2-3 × 10-15 | controlled | 60 sec | BW |

**Notes on A:** BB Back Squat high-bar, descend to parallel only. Triceps Pushdown replaces Overhead DB Triceps Ext as of Apr 25 — OH ext caused elbow strain Session 1. Use rope, elbows pinned at sides, full extension at the bottom, controlled return. **Yellow flag as of Apr 27** — keep weight conservative (20-25 lb), 3-sec eccentric. DB Curl replaces EZ-Bar Curl as of Apr 27 (loading hassle).

### Workout B — Vertical Push + Hip Hinge Emphasis

| # | Exercise | Sets × Reps | Tempo | Rest | Starting Weight |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Seated DB Shoulder Press (neutral) | 3 × 8-12 | 3-0-1-0 | 2 min | 25-30 lb/hand |
| 2 | Romanian Deadlift (barbell) | 3 × 8-12 | 3-0-1-0 | 2 min | 95-115 lb |
| 3 | Lat Pulldown OR Chin-Up | 3 × 6-10 | 2-0-1-1 | 2 min | 90-100 lb / BW |
| 4 | Flat DB Bench Press (elbows ~45°) | 3 × 10-15 | 3-0-1-0 | 90 sec | 35-40 lb/hand |
| 5 | Heel-Elevated Goblet Squat | 2 × 10-15 | 3-0-1-0 | 90 sec | 40-55 lb DB |
| 6 | Cable Face Pull | 3 × 15-20 | 2-1-1-1 | 60 sec | 30-50 lb |
| 7 | DB Lateral Raise | 2 × 12-20 | 2-1-1-0 | 60 sec | 10-12 lb |
| 8 | DB Hammer Curl | 2 × 10-12 | 2-0-1-0 | 90 sec | 20-25 lb/hand |
| 9 | Triceps Pushdown (rope) | 2 × 10-15 | 2-1-1-0 | 90 sec | 20-30 lb |

**Notes on B:** Neutral-grip shoulder press is best for shoulder longevity. Barbell RDL — hinge, don't squat; stop at hamstring stretch. Heel-Elevated Goblet Squat replaces DB Reverse Lunge as of Apr 25. Heels on a 1-2 inch plate or wedge, DB or KB at chest, descend to parallel, slow eccentric. Same depth rule as the back squat: stop at parallel. Face pulls = shoulder insurance. Pushdown swap as in Workout A.

### Workout C — Optional "Pump & Recovery" Day

Higher reps, lighter loads, almost no joint stress. Take this instead of a scheduled A or B if you're worn out — do not add it on top.

| # | Exercise | Sets × Reps | Tempo | Rest | Weight |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Push-Up (feet on floor) | 3 × 15 | 3-0-1-0 | 90 sec | BW |
| 2 | Single-Arm DB Row | 3 × 15/arm | 2-0-1-1 | 90 sec | 30 lb |
| 3 | Heel-Elevated Goblet Squat | 3 × 15 | 3-0-1-0 | 90 sec | 50 lb |
| 4 | Seated Cable Row (neutral grip) | 3 × 15 | 2-1-1-1 | 90 sec | 60 lb |
| 5 | DB Lateral Raise | 3 × 20 | controlled | 60 sec | 8 lb |
| 6 | DB Hammer Curl | 2 × 12 | controlled | 60 sec | 20 lb |
| 7 | Triceps Rope Pushdown | 2 × 15 | controlled | 60 sec | 20 lb |
| 8 | Lying Leg Raise | 2 × 15 | controlled | 60 sec | BW |

Workout C uses the same prescription every time — no progression. Its job is to keep you moving without cost.

## 4. Progression Rules

**Double progression** — most robust, injury-friendly progression method.

1. Start at the low end of the rep range at the prescribed weight.
2. Each session, try to add reps until all sets hit the TOP of the range.
3. Once all working sets hit the top, add 2.5-5 lb next session and work back to the bottom.
4. Repeat.

**The 10% rule:** Never increase total weight on any exercise by more than 10% in a single jump.

**Deload:** Every 4 weeks, run one deload week — half the sets, ~70% of normal weights. Non-negotiable.

## 5. Warm-Up Protocol (Do Not Skip)

**General (2 min):** 30 sec jumping jacks, 10 arm circles each direction, 10 BW squats, 10 scap pull-ups, 10 cat-cow.

**Specific (before main compound):** Empty bar × 10-12 → 50% × 6-8 → 75% × 3-5 → working weight.

## 6. Mesocycle Structure (4-Week Cycles)

| Week | Volume | Intensity (RIR) | Goal |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | All sets as prescribed | 3 RIR | Groove movement patterns, feel joints |
| 2 | Same sets | 2 RIR | Modest weight increase where earned |
| 3 | +1 set on main compounds | 1-2 RIR | Peak accumulation |
| 4 | Deload: half sets, -30% weight | 4+ RIR | Recover, consolidate |

**Volume targets per muscle/week (by end of wk 3):**

| Muscle | Sets/Week | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Chest | 10-12 | Distributed across A and B |
| Back | 12-14 | Rows + pulldowns + chins |
| Quads | 8-10 | BB Squat + goblet squat |
| Hamstrings | 6-9 | Primarily BB RDL + DB RDL |
| Side delts | 8-10 | Lateral raises every session |
| Rear delts | 3-6 | Face pulls in B |
| Biceps | 6-8 | Direct + indirect from rows |
| Triceps | 6-8 | Direct + indirect from presses |
| Abs | 4-6 | Enough for visibility at 15% BF |

*Calves dropped from program Apr 27 — not training them.*

## 7. Form Cues for Joint-Friendly Staples

**DB Bench / Incline DB Bench:** Elbows tracked at ~45° from torso, NEVER flared to 90°. DBs start at shoulder width, press slightly together at the top. Lower until elbows are level with torso, not below. 3-second descent.

**BB Back Squat:** High-bar position. Feet shoulder-width, toes ~15-20° out. Brace HARD before descending. Stop at parallel — going below is what historically wrecked your knees. Drive up with chest proud, knees over toes. 3-sec descent, no bounce. *Apr 27 watch: caused lower back discomfort that climbed across days. Reassess in Mesocycle 2 — may swap permanently to goblet squat.*

**Romanian Deadlift (BB or DB):** Soft knee bend, hinge from hips. Bar/DBs slide down legs. Stop at hamstring stretch OR mid-shin, whichever first. NOT a deadlift — don't touch the floor.

**Neutral-Grip DB Shoulder Press:** Palms face each other throughout. DBs at shoulder level → straight up. Reduce ROM if any pinch — don't push through.

**Lat Pulldown:** Use whatever handle you've got set up. Lean back slightly (10-15°). Pull to upper chest. 1-sec pause at bottom, controlled eccentric.

**Heel-Elevated Goblet Squat:** Heels on a 1-2 inch plate, book, or wedge. Hold DB or KB at chest. Brace, sit straight down (heel elevation lets knees travel forward without pinch). Stop at parallel, same as back squat. Slow eccentric.

**Triceps Pushdown:** Rope attachment preferred. Elbows pinned at sides, do not let them flare forward. Full extension at the bottom; squeeze. Controlled return. Easier on elbow tendons than overhead extension or skullcrushers. *Apr 27 watch: cranky elbows at 25 lb. Try 20 lb + 3-sec eccentric next session before swapping.*

## 8. Travel / Away Gym Adaptations

Added Apr 25 after a UK travel session forced significant substitutions. Use this as a reference when away from your home gym.

**Substitution priorities (in order):** (1) Preserve the movement pattern (push, pull, hinge, squat). (2) Preserve the rep range and RIR. (3) Match the limb/joint angle if possible (e.g. neutral grip stays neutral). (4) Weight match is least important — use what's available and adjust on first set.

| Missing | Substitute | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Barbell (squat) | Heel-elev goblet squat OR machine leg press | Goblet squat caps at DB max; add leg ext if needed |
| Barbell (RDL) | DB RDL (heaviest available) | Capped by DB max; do more reps to compensate |
| Cable lat pulldown | Functional trainer (e.g. Kinesis) high-pulley with rope/V | Calibrate by feel — use RIR target as anchor |
| Cable face pull | Functional trainer high-pulley, rope to forehead | Same |
| Cable triceps pushdown | Functional trainer high-pulley with rope | Same |
| Pull-up bar (leg raises) | Lying leg raise | 1:1 swap |
| Quad accessory if no BB squat | Selectorized leg extension | Don't snap into lockout; stop short of full ext |

**Calibrating unfamiliar resistance machines (functional trainers, etc):** First set is a probe. Pick a conservative level, do 5 reps, stop. If you could do 10+ more, bump up 2 levels and start set 1 properly. Adjust between sets, not within. Log the level + reps + RIR — by session 3 you'll have your own table.

## 9. Nutrition — No Counting Required

**Daily protein:** 150-180g (0.8-1g per lb bodyweight). 30-45g per meal × 4 meals = 150g easy.

**Four behavioral rules:**
1. Protein first at every meal — before carbs, before fat, eat 30-45g protein.
2. Half your plate vegetables or fruit at lunch and dinner.
3. Cap liquid calories at one per day (coffee w/ milk OR protein shake OR alcohol — pick one).
4. Don't eat after dinner unless it's casein-rich (cottage cheese, Greek yogurt).

**Weekly weigh-in:** same day, same time, first thing AM after bathroom. Target 0.25-0.5 lb loss/week. Faster than 1 lb/week — add food. Flat weight + strength up + waist shrinking = textbook recomp.

**Supplements worth taking:** Creatine monohydrate 5g/day; protein powder for hitting target; vitamin D3 2000 IU/day; fish oil 2-3g EPA+DHA/day. Skip everything else.

## 10. Cardio / Walking

**Goal:** 8,000-10,000 steps/day on most days. Plenty for body composition. Walking doesn't interfere with hypertrophy.

Stroller walks count 100%. Break into multiple walks if needed (20 min × 3 > 60 min × 1 for adherence). No structured cardio required. If you want one hard session/week — 20-min EMOM of 10 KB swings, or 20-min Zone 2 bike. No running — you're 186 lb, returning, with knee history.

## 11. Logging & Analysis System

**Hevy (per workout):** exercise, sets, reps, weight (already done), plus: (1) RIR per set in the RPE field (RIR 3 = RPE 7, 2 = 8, 1 = 9, 0 = 10). (2) Joint/tendon discomfort in notes — format "location - severity/10 - when". (3) Subjective session rating in workout description (1-10).

**Weekly metrics (Wed AM, before Session 1):** bodyweight, waist at navel, average sleep, average energy 1-10, steps/day average, persistent niggles.

**Upload cadence:** every 4 weeks (after deload), export Hevy CSV + weekly metrics CSV → ask Claude to analyze. Mid-cycle check-ins fine for specific issues.

## 12. Red Flags — Back Off Immediately

- Joint/tendon pain >4/10 during a set → stop that exercise for the session, sub something else.
- Pain that lingers >48h after → drop the offending exercise, replace with lower-stress variant for 2 weeks.
- Morning stiffness worsening day-to-day → tendon hasn't recovered, take an extra rest day.
- Sleep crashing <6h for 3+ nights → deload this week regardless of where you are in the cycle.
- Strength dropping across multiple exercises 2+ weeks → under-recovered, deload.

**Tendinopathy rule:** pain 24h post-exercise should stay the same or drop. If it climbs, you did too much.

## Quick-Reference Card

**Training:** 3x/week, alternate A and B. C = optional swap. 45-60 min, splittable. Compounds: 2-3 RIR, slow eccentric. Isolations: 1-2 RIR. Deload week 4.

**Progression:** Double progression. Add reps to top of range, then add 2.5-5 lb. Never >10% jumps.

**Nutrition:** 150-180g protein/day. Protein first, half-plate veggies, cap liquid calories, no after-dinner grazing. 8,000-10,000 steps/day. Creatine 5g, fish oil 2-3g, D3 2000 IU.

**Data:** Hevy with RIR + pain notes. Weekly CSV (Wed AM). Upload every 4 weeks.

**Red flags:** Joint pain >4/10 → stop. Pain >48h → sub. Stiffness worsening → extra rest. Strength dropping 2+ weeks → deload.
